title: Fundamentals of Compute
description: A rigorous 20-week graduate-level course covering computation from transistor physics to supercomputer architecture. Master digital logic, processor design, memory hierarchies, GPU programming, FPGA-based high-frequency trading, and high-performance computing. Students build progressively complex systems in C and C++ through 6 guided projects, culminating in a capstone that pushes the boundaries of computational systems.

weeks.title: Transistors and Logic Gates
weeks.description: Semiconductor physics, MOSFET operation, CMOS logic gates, Boolean algebra, and Karnaugh maps. Introduction to C programming with bitwise operators.

weeks.title: Combinational Circuits and Arithmetic
weeks.description: Number representation, IEEE 754 floating point, adder circuits, carry-lookahead, Booth's multiplication, ALU design. C pointers and arrays.

weeks.title: Sequential Circuits and State Machines
weeks.description: Latches, flip-flops, timing analysis, registers, counters, finite state machines (Moore and Mealy), microprogrammed control. C dynamic memory allocation.

weeks.title: Memory Technologies and Storage
weeks.description: SRAM 6T cell, DRAM 1T1C, DDR5 and HBM3 specifications, SSD architecture, NVMe, storage hierarchy. C structs and file I/O.

lessons.title: Flash Storage, SSDs, and the Storage Hierarchy

weeks.title: Instruction Set Architecture
weeks.description: ISA design philosophy, CISC vs RISC, RISC-V RV32I complete instruction set with bit-level encoding, assembly programming, compilation pipeline. C preprocessor and Makefiles.

lessons.title: Instruction Set Architecture: CISC, RISC, and RISC-V
RISC-V Assembly Programming and the Compilation Pipeline

weeks.title: Processor Datapath Design
weeks.description: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath with signal-by-signal trace, multi-cycle design with FSM controller, microprogrammed control. C libraries and linking.

weeks.title: Pipelining and Branch Prediction
weeks.description: 5-stage pipeline, data hazards and forwarding, control hazards, branch prediction from 1-bit to TAGE and neural predictors, Amdahl's Law. C++ classes and RAII.

lessons.title: Branch Prediction: From Saturating Counters to Neural Predictors

weeks.title: Cache Hierarchies and Virtual Memory
weeks.description: Cache design from direct-mapped to fully-associative, AMAT analysis, multi-level caches, virtual memory with Sv39 page tables, TLBs, roofline model. C++ templates and STL.

lessons.title: Cache Design and the Memory Hierarchy
Virtual Memory, TLBs, and the Roofline Model
Problem Set 8: Caches and Virtual Memory

weeks.title: Out-of-Order Execution and Advanced Processors
weeks.description: Tomasulo's algorithm, register renaming, reorder buffer, speculative execution, VLIW, SMT, power wall, chiplet architectures. C++ smart pointers and move semantics.

lessons.title: Speculative Execution, VLIW, SMT, and the Power Wall

weeks.title: Midterm and Project Review
weeks.description: Comprehensive midterm exam covering weeks 1-9. Review of Project 1 (Logic Simulator) and Project 2 (RISC-V Processor) final milestones.

lessons.title: Project Architecture Review and Performance Analysis

weeks.title: Multicore Processors and Coherence
weeks.description: Cache coherence (MESI protocol with full state diagrams), directory-based coherence, false sharing, synchronization primitives, lock-free programming, CXL, NUMA. C++ concurrency.

weeks.title: SIMD, Vectors, and Matrix Operations
weeks.description: Flynn's taxonomy, SIMD and vector processing (AVX, SVE, RISC-V V), tiled GEMM derivation, Strassen's algorithm, systolic arrays, BLAS levels. C++ operator overloading.

lessons.title: Matrix Operations: From GEMM to Systolic Arrays
Problem Set 11: SIMD and Matrix Ops

weeks.title: GPU Architecture
weeks.description: GPU hardware internals (SM structure, warp/SIMT execution, memory hierarchy, coalescing, occupancy), architecture evolution from Kepler to Blackwell, GPU vs CPU design trade-offs.

lessons.title: GPU Hardware: Streaming Multiprocessors and SIMT

weeks.title: GPU Programming and Optimization
weeks.description: CUDA programming model, parallel reduction (5 optimization levels), scan, tiled GEMM, radix sort, multi-GPU with NVLink, ring-AllReduce algorithm.

lessons.title: CUDA Programming: Kernels, Threads, and Memory
GPU Optimization: Reduction, Scan, GEMM, and Multi-GPU

weeks.title: FPGA Architecture and HDL
weeks.description: FPGA internals (CLB, LUT, BRAM, DSP, routing), FPGA vs ASIC vs CPU, Verilog HDL (behavioral, structural, synthesis patterns, testbenches), High-Level Synthesis with Vitis HLS.

lessons.title: FPGA Architecture: LUTs, BRAMs, and Programmable Logic

weeks.title: FPGA for High-Frequency Trading
weeks.description: FPGA in HFT: FAST protocol decoding, hardware order books, tick-to-trade pipelines, fixed-point arithmetic, colocation, sub-microsecond latency. The complete FPGA trading system.

lessons.title: The FPGA Trading Pipeline: From Wire to Order
Hardware Order Books, Risk Checks, and Colocation

weeks.title: Supercomputer Architecture and HPC
weeks.description: Top supercomputers (Frontier, El Capitan), interconnect topologies (fat tree, dragonfly, torus), MPI collectives, distributed memory, parallel I/O, power and cooling.

lessons.title: Supercomputer Architecture: From Frontier to El Capitan

weeks.title: Systems Programming and Performance Engineering
weeks.description: System calls, scheduling, memory management, I/O models, zero-copy, NUMA, huge pages, CPU isolation for trading, profiling, cache-line optimization, SIMD intrinsics, benchmarking.

lessons.title: Systems Programming: Kernel Interfaces and Memory Management
Performance Engineering: Profiling, Optimization, and Benchmarking

weeks.title: Quantitative Finance Computing
weeks.description: Trading technology stack, GPU for risk (Monte Carlo, Black-Scholes), FPGA for execution, kernel bypass networking, compute-intensive finance: Monte Carlo on GPU, fixed-point Black-Scholes, real-time risk, covariance and PCA.

lessons.title: The Quant Computing Stack: Data, Signal, Execution, Risk
Compute-Intensive Finance: Monte Carlo, Risk, and Real-Time Analytics

weeks.title: Future of Compute and Final Exam
weeks.description: 3D integration, chiplets, photonic computing, processing-in-memory, neuromorphic computing, quantum computing current state, post-quantum cryptography for finance. Capstone project and comprehensive final exam.

lessons.title: The Future of Compute: Photonics, PIM, Neuromorphic, and Quantum
Capstone Project Specification and Course Synthesis
